page.content: The data presented in the accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of the relationship between various institutional characteristics and their corresponding outcomes in the context of liver triglyceride levels, measured in millimoles per gram of liver (TG).

lines.text: The data presented in the accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of the relationship between various
institutional characteristics and their corresponding outcomes in the context of liver triglyceride levels, measured in millimoles

page.html:
InstitutionFieldNumber of interviews
Public UniversityHumanities8 (4 men, 4 women)
Public UniversityArchitecture8 (4 men, 4 women)
Public UniversityTelecommunications Engineering8 (4 men, 4 women)
Research CentreEnvironmental Sciences4 (2 men, 2 women)
Research CentreBiomedicine8 (4 men, 4 women)

ModelFeatures SelectedLOOCV Misclassification Rate (%)
Logistic Regression - Forward Stepwise4 – 931
Greedy - LDA10 – 2031
Genetic algorithm - LDA1028

Robust feature selection - LDA5 – 1131

page.content: Specifically, the table details the number of interviews conducted across different institutions, alongside their respective fields of study, showcasing a diverse range of research environments. For instance, a public university in the humanities conducted eight interviews, evenly divided between male and female participants, which reflects a balanced consideration of gender in the study. The model employed to analyze the data is crucial for understanding the efficiency of feature selection, as indicated by the column "Features Selected." Furthermore, the misclassification rates presented, particularly the LOOCV (Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation) Misclassification Rate (%), highlight the accuracy and reliability of the predictive models utilized in this research. These metrics collectively underscore the significance of institutional context and methodological rigor in exploring the determinants of liver triglyceride levels.
